title: unit.v1
description: The DISPATCH — the one backend object (one run of an agent), composed of the seven primitives. The Scheduler fires it; the Runtime spawns an isolated container, mounts the granted workspace LIST + brokers creds; the Agent (runner) runs from a start point and streams on its derived Stream `unit:<id>:out`. The envelope carries WHO it acts for and who launched it (identity: subject · launcher · signed token), WHICH agent runs (runner), WHICH workspaces it mounts at what access (workspaces[{id,mode}] — mode is the write-access source of truth), WHICH tools it may call (tools — each auto|gate per its tool.v1 descriptor), WHY it fired (trigger — informs the DERIVED input-trust), what it is grounded in (context), and WHAT to do (start: an entry point or a resumed session). `trust` (all-ro && irreversible-gated) and `output` (`unit:<id>:out`) are DERIVED, never stored. There is no `lifecycle` — the Runtime reaps on idle and continuity is the session file in the workspace. No tenancy beyond subject (ADR-0003). Supersedes the flat MVP shape and the meeting-only invoke.v1.

Trigger.description: Why the dispatch fired — an input to the Scheduler, recorded for provenance + to DERIVE input-trust (message = you, in chat ⇒ trusted ⇒ rw; event/transcription from an external source ⇒ untrusted ⇒ propose-only). message = a user turn (chat, now); scheduled = a schedule entry fired (routine); event = an Integration published an event (e.g. email.received); transcription = a live transcript stream beat / session start (the in-meeting agent).

Mode.description: Workspace access mode — the SOURCE OF TRUTH for write access (governance). ro = materialized read-only; rw = synced back to the bucket on commit.

Workspace.description: One granted workspace mount: a workspace.v1 git-folder `id` (the store resolves it to a bucket location) + the access `mode`. A dispatch mounts a LIST — typically system (ro) + company (ro) + user (rw).

id.description: the workspace.v1 id (e.g. the subject for the personal workspace, or `system`/`company`)

Identity.description: The chain of custody. subject = the principal the agent acts ON BEHALF OF (data + quota owner). launcher = WHAT triggered this dispatch (the user, a schedule entry, or an Integration) — it must hold a signed delegation grant to act as the subject. token = the short-lived signed dispatch token (subject + launcher + scope) the Identity service mints; absent at author time, injected by the Runtime at spawn; every boundary verifies it, never the agent.

subject.description: identity.v1 subject = the PERSON = quota owner + cred-brokerage key (e.g. u_jane)

launcher.description: who/what triggered the dispatch, e.g. user:u_jane | schedule:<id> | integration:meetings

token.description: the minted short-lived signed dispatch token; injected at spawn, verified at every boundary

MeetingRef.description: Which meeting this dispatch is grounded in (context.kind=meeting) — referenced by id, never by importing meetings code (the meetings⊥agent boundary). The ids match transcript.v1; the agent consumes transcript.v1 by schema off `tc:meeting:{id}:mutable`.

SourceRef.description: An OPAQUE reference to a non-meeting context source (an email, a doc, a generic uri) that a tool resolves — never the bytes. Keeps email/calendar/tasks as Integrations, not platform domains.

uri.description: an opaque source uri, e.g. mailbox://user/INBOX/<id>

Context.description: What the dispatch is grounded in. `kind` is the discriminator; `none` is a bare chat with no external context. For a live meeting, `meeting` carries the ids the agent uses to consume the transcript stream.

EntryPoint.description: A fresh start: an inline ask (chat message / inline plan) and/or a workspace path to a plan doc. At least one of `inline` | `path`.

path.description: a workspace path to a plan doc, e.g. plan/morning-brief.md

SessionRef.description: A resumed start: a path to the agent's session file in the (rw) workspace. A fresh container resumes instantly from it — continuity is a file, not a warm container.

ref.description: a workspace path to the session file, e.g. .claude/.session

Start.description: What to do: EXACTLY ONE of an `entrypoint` (a fresh task) or a `session` (a resumed conversation).

Invocation.description: One dispatch. The dispatcher emits it (from any trigger source); the Scheduler fires it; the Runtime spawns it. `output` (= unit:<id>:out) and `trust` are derived, not stored.

runner.description: which agent CLI runner drives the turn (claude-code is the first; aider/others plug in)

workspaces.description: the granted workspace mounts (id + mode); mode is the write-access source of truth

tools.description: the scoped toolbelt (tool.v1 names); each resolves to auto|gate via its descriptor — the --allowedTools allow-set + the gate set
